content:
# Acoustic Impedance

**Summary**: A physical property of rock that is the product of density and velocity.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002]] used acoustic-impedance as a common currency to link seismic data to petrophysical properties.

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] used acoustic impedance as an intermediate property derived from seismic inversion to predict porosity.

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] used acoustic impedance (AI) as a primary seismic attribute for fluid identification.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] used to describe the impact of geological features on seismic waves.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] used acoustic impedance to characterize reservoir fluids.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] used acoustic-impedance to evaluate well log data.

- [[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023]] used acoustic impedance as an intermediate step in the inversion process to derive velocity profiles.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] used acoustic impedance to model the seismic response of bioturbated strata.

## Scientific Context

- [[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002]] It allows for a more direct relationship between seismic data and reservoir properties like porosity.

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] used acoustic impedance as an intermediate property derived from seismic inversion to predict porosity.

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] used acoustic impedance (AI) as a primary seismic attribute for fluid identification.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] used to describe the impact of geological features on seismic waves.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] used acoustic impedance to characterize reservoir fluids.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] used acoustic-impedance to evaluate well log data.

- [[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023]] used acoustic impedance as an intermediate step in the inversion process to derive velocity profiles.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] used acoustic impedance to model the seismic response of bioturbated strata.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002]] derived from seismic data via inversion processes.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Impedance is an ideal basis for the application of modern volume interpretation methods because it is a layer property. ([[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002, p. 39]])

- Acoustic impedance was used as a primary indicator for lithology and porosity in the study. ([[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015, p. 29]])

- Inversion of AI, P-wave velocity, density, and compressional modulus resulted in a percent error of almost 0% for gas, live oil, and brine. ([[2016-ali|Ali, 2016, p. 14]])

- Not stated in source ([[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]])

- Hydrocarbon-filled reservoirs are characterized by lower acoustic impedance than surrounding water-filled rocks. ([[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019, p. 19]])

- P-wave impedance was used as a primary indicator for mineralogy and porosity. ([[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020, p. 43]])

- The corridor stack is deconvolved with a generated wavelet to obtain a reflectivity series, which is then inverted for relative acoustic impedance. ([[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023, p. 30]])

- A 100 Hz Ricker wavelet was convolved with an impedance model derived from velocity and density data. ([[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024, p. 65]])
